access.guest_fallback_message: Detailed explanations are available for logged-in Saola students only.

q6.explanation: Cần điền/chọn thông tin đúng từ bài đọc. Ở đoạn C, bài viết nêu: "The quantity of inflammable material around a fire is known as “the fuel load” and is determined by the amount of available fuel per unit area, usually measured in tons per acre. The dryness of the fuel also influences how fires behave. Dry fuel burns quickly and makes the fires much harder to control.". Cụm the fuel load khớp với yêu cầu câu hỏi và đúng giới hạn từ nếu đề có quy định.
q6.evidence: The quantity of inflammable material around a fire is known as “the fuel load” and is determined by the amount of available fuel per unit area, usually measured in tons per acre. The dryness of the fuel also influences how fires behave. Dry fuel burns quickly and makes the fires much harder to control._

q7.explanation: Cần điền/chọn thông tin đúng từ bài đọc. Ở đoạn D, bài viết nêu: "The sun heats and dries sticks, trees and underbrush, turning them into potential fuel. Higher temperatures can cause fuels to ignite, burn more quickly and add to the speed of a wildfire’s spread. Consequently, wildfires tend to rage in the afternoon, when temperatures are at their hottest.". Cụm in the afternoon khớp với yêu cầu câu hỏi và đúng giới hạn từ nếu đề có quy định.
q7.evidence: The sun heats and dries sticks, trees and underbrush, turning them into potential fuel. Higher temperatures can cause fuels to ignite, burn more quickly and add to the speed of a wildfire’s spread. Consequently, wildfires tend to rage in the afternoon, when temperatures are at their hottest._

q8.explanation: Cần điền/chọn thông tin đúng từ bài đọc. Ở đoạn D, bài viết nêu: "Fires also create winds of their own that can be up to ten times faster than the ambient wind. Winds can spread embers that generate additional fires, an event known as “spotting”. Winds also change the course of fires, and gusts can take flames up into trees, starting “crown fires”.". Cụm embers khớp với yêu cầu câu hỏi và đúng giới hạn từ nếu đề có quy định.
q8.evidence: Fires also create winds of their own that can be up to ten times faster than the ambient wind. Winds can spread embers that generate additional fires, an event known as “spotting”. Winds also change the course of fires, and gusts can take flames up into trees, starting “crown fires”._

q9.explanation: Cần điền/chọn thông tin đúng từ bài đọc. Ở đoạn F, bài viết nêu: "As their name suggests, smokejumpers jump out of aircraft to reach smaller fires situated in inaccessible regions. Their aim is to contain these smaller fires before they turn into bigger ones. As well as constructing firebreaks and putting water and retardant (a red chemical containing phosphate fertiliser) on fires, fire fighters also create “backfires” which burn towards the main fire, incinerating any potential fuel in the wildfire’s path.". Cụm backfires khớp với yêu cầu câu hỏi và đúng giới hạn từ nếu đề có quy định.
q9.evidence: As their name suggests, smokejumpers jump out of aircraft to reach smaller fires situated in inaccessible regions. Their aim is to contain these smaller fires before they turn into bigger ones. As well as constructing firebreaks and putting water and retardant (a red chemical containing phosphate fertiliser) on fires, fire fighters also create “backfires” which burn towards the main fire, incinerating any potential fuel in the wildfire’s path._
